title: invAIriant Audit Report
description: Machine-readable form of an audit report. Interpretation rules: a high average lens score never cancels an S0/S1 finding; verdicts derive from the severity model, not from score arithmetic; rejected hypotheses are kept, not deleted.

type.description: Project type from invairiant.config.yml, e.g. infra-service.

phase.description: Roadmap phase or milestone, if the project uses phases.

scope.description: What was audited and, just as important, what was not.

provenance.description: Binds this report to the evidence bundle and commit it was built from. Copied from the bundle's own `provenance` block (see evidence-bundle.schema.json) so a later step — the CI Action — can prove the report was produced for THIS commit from THIS bundle and wasn't hand-edited after the audit. Integrity, not judgment: the CLI still never decides whether a finding is real. Optional today; `validate-report` warns when a report with findings omits it, ahead of a later release requiring it.

commit_sha.description: The commit the audited scope was resolved at (bundle.provenance.commit_sha).

scope_hash.description: sha256 over {kind, target, sorted files} of the resolved scope.

bundle_hash.description: sha256 of the evidence bundle this report was built from (bundle.provenance.bundle_hash).

inputs_reviewed.description: Files, diffs, docs, CI runs, logs, and tool outputs actually reviewed.

participants.description: Humans and AI agents, with roles, e.g. 'jsmith (security auditor)', 'claude (lens auditor: parnas, turing)'.

verdict.description: One-line justification. Must reference evidence, not taste.

evidence_refs.description: Pointers backing the score: file:lines, finding ids, test names, doc sections.

observations.description: Evidence-light items worth recording. Never merged into findings without evidence.

hypotheses.description: Proposed explanations that were not verified, including candidates rejected by evidence verification.

blocking.description: True if this action gates the merge/release/phase transition.

evidence_appendix.description: Longer excerpts, command outputs, and reproduction notes.

follow_up.description: What evidence would promote or retire this item.

rejected_reason.description: For hypotheses rejected by evidence verification: why.
